reasoning: The Lean signature encodes exactly the informal claim. `q : ℕ` with `q.Prime` and `q ≡ 3 [ZMOD 4]` faithfully captures "prime q ∈ ℤ with q ≡ 3 mod 4" (primes in ℤ are, up to sign, positive primes). `GaussianInt ⧸ span ({↑q} : Set GaussianInt)` is ℤ[i]/(q), the quotient by the principal ideal generated by q. The conclusion is a conjunction: (1) `IsField` captures "is a field" (the Mathlib `IsField` structure requires two distinct elements, commutativity, and multiplicative inverses — exactly a field), and (2) the existential `∃ finR : Fintype (...), Fintype.card _ finR = q^2` captures "has q² elements" (the type is finite and its cardinality is q²). The Fintype cardinality is independent of the chosen Fintype instance, so this is equivalent to stating "|ℤ[i]/(q)| = q²". No hypotheses are added or dropped beyond what the informal statement gives.
